task_statement: P7d of BRICK 6-surface audit repair: close S6-F4 Easy Building declaration ergonomics by productizing natural-language big-work route shape over official brick build / brick build --graph, without --large, new engine, support-owned authority, or preset-only shortcut.
selection_rule: manual graph because S6-F4 requires the big-work route shape itself: task intake -> design fan-out -> design synthesis/plan confirm -> parallel work lanes -> lane QA -> final QA fan-out/fan-in -> closure. A linear preset would not preserve the phase contract.

nodes.work_statement:
Task-intake/design intake for P7d. Read the objective, P7/S6 source audit docs, P7c closure, and current public route docs. Return the exact customer-facing task fields and graph-declaration ergonomics gap for S6-F4. No source edits.

STRICT SCOPE: phase:P7d product-route / Easy Building declaration ergonomics ONLY. Close S6-F4 by making the natural-language big-work route easy to understand and checker-pinned: make X -> task intake -> design fan-out -> design QA/axis QA -> closure plan-confirm -> parallel dev lanes -> lane QA -> fan-in -> final code/axis/evidence QA -> closure -> official brick build / brick build --graph. Do NOT add or revive --large, _p3_easy_large, --dev-lanes, lane_return, a new engine, support-owned route choice, scheduler/queue/retry runtime, or a preset-only shortcut. If a preset is mentioned, it is only a reusable route explanation when it preserves the phase contract; the official route remains declared graph_packet / preset_task over brick build. Keep public/operator language build()/brick build only. fire(), assemble(), run_building_plan, launch_assembled_building, and full internal Building Plan are internal/advanced/debug/support terms, not the ordinary customer route. Always separate observed_evidence / narrowly_proven / not_proven / next Movement candidate. Checker/profile green is support evidence only.
